title: sign_request API accepts private key as plain &str
description: In signatures.rs:17, `sign_request()` takes `private_key_pem: &str`. At every call site (delivery.rs:183, server.rs:481), the private key is fetched from the DB as a plain `String` and passed directly. There is no type-level enforcement that callers treat this as secret material. This means the key can be accidentally passed to logging macros, format strings, or other functions that accept `&str`. Fix: define a `struct PrivateKeyPem(Zeroizing<String>)` that does not derive `Debug`, `Display`, or `Serialize`, and change the function signature to accept `&PrivateKeyPem`. This makes accidental logging a compile error rather than a runtime incident.
close_reason: P2-P3: RateLimiter::new returns Self, remaining items accepted as known tech debt or tracked in hardening epic
